---
title: "Signal decomposition"
sidebar: home
---

The Rayleigh-corrected reflectance is obtained from the TOA reflectance as:

$$\rho_{\text{rc}} = \rho_{\text{TOA}} / t_g - \rho_r$$

[Alternatively](reference/rayleigh.qmd), it can also be divided by the Rayleigh diffuse transmittance:

$$\rho_{\text{rc}}^\prime = \frac{\rho_{\text{TOA}} / t_g - \rho_r}{t_r}$$

+---------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Symbol              | Description                                                                  |
+=====================+==============================================================================+
| $\rho_{\text{TOA}}$ | Top-of-atmosphere reflectance:                                               |
|                     | $\rho_{\text{TOA}} = \pi \, L_{\text{TOA}} / (F_0 \, \mu_0)$                 |
+---------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| $L_{\text{TOA}}$    | TOA radiance                                                                 |
+---------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| $F_0$               | Extraterrestrial solar irradiance                                            |
+---------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| $\mu_0$             | Cosine of the solar zenith angle                                             |
+---------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| $\rho_r$            | [Rayleigh reflectance](rayleigh.qmd)                                         |
+---------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| $t_g$               | [Gaseous absorption transmittance](gaseous.qmd) along the downward + upward  |
|                     | (solar and sensor) path                                                      |
+---------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| $t_r$               | Rayleigh diffuse transmittance along the solar+sensor view path              |
+---------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
